item.description:
First release of Valv for macOS: sync your folders, share them, and keep every version.
## Sync
- mounted folders appear in Finder and materialize on demand, so a folder costs nothing until you open a file in it - edits to a large file upload only the parts that changed - every file keeps its version history, and any version can be restored - conflicting edits become a conflict copy beside the original, never a silent overwrite - deletes, moves, and renames propagate correctly, including to a Mac that was offline when they happened
## Sharing
- share a folder from Finder, or from the Manage Folders window - invite someone by email and they accept in the browser with their own account - hand over an access key to set up a machine that has no Valv account, such as a home server or a VPS - a machine you hand a key to cannot share the folder onward - read-only sharing gives read access without write
## The rest
- menu-bar app showing daemon health and every folder's sync state - in-app updates - a `valv` command line for scripting, with `--json` on every command

Sharing from Finder, and a File Provider that no longer hangs or loses its domain.
## macOS app
- Share a folder straight from Finder: a new Valv Finder Extension adds a share action, backed by an in-app share window - Mounted folders now present as symlinks, so Finder and command-line tools resolve them consistently - Finder enumeration no longer hangs: the root container is answered locally instead of falling through to a node lookup that returned `node_not_found` - The File Provider domain is reconciled on every signed-in launch, so an install whose signed-in state outlived its domain repairs itself instead of showing an empty mount - Onboarding now stops on a File Provider registration failure rather than landing you signed in with nothing mounted - Reworked menu-bar dropdown and sign-out flow - Onboarding hero uses the brand icon - Requires `valvd` 0.2.0 or newer: v0.1.0 registers none of the `/fp/*` routes the File Provider extension calls, so the old floor passed the compatibility gate and every Finder operation then 404'd
## Daemon (`valvd`) and CLI (`valv`)
- New `GET /nodes/by-path` route, with a matching `nodeIdForPath` method in DaemonKit - Cross-folder moves are rejected with `409 cross_folder_move_rejected` instead of orphaning the node from both folders - Config errors now point at `valv auth login`, which is what actually writes `device_token` — the old hint dead-ended at `daemon install`, whose template leaves the token empty
